title: GLM GPU Witness Record
description: Machine-readable record of an on-device GLM-5.2 (glm_moe_dsa) pure-kernel GPU witness run, parsed from the dgx_glm_gpu_witness.sh log. Each per-test cosine is the output of a `go test -tags cuda` gate that FAILS unless the cosine clears its floor, so the per-test rc (and overall rc) is a kernel-enforced assertion, not a hand-transcribed self-report. NOTE on DOS: `dos verify` binds the COMMIT that carries this record (git ancestry + stamp grammar); it does not open or re-check the cosine number. content_sha256 anchors the record to the exact raw log it was parsed from.

utc.description: ISO 8601 UTC stamp, injected by the caller (never read from the box wall clock, for determinism)

machine_id.description: Catalog machine id. 'dgx' for the raw private record; 'a100' for the scrubbed public rollup.

node.description: Hardware hostname (private only; DROPPED in the public a100 rollup). A hardware name -- keep out of public *.md prose.

head_sha.description: origin/main short sha that was cloned + built on the box (git rev-parse --short HEAD)

head_subject.description: Commit subject of head_sha (optional; populated when available)

arch.description: CUDA compute capability the kernels were built for, e.g. sm_80

environment.description: Generic, scrub-safe environment facts (no hardware hostname)

path_exercised.description: Human description of what runs on the GPU for this test

cosine.description: Device-vs-host cosine; null for set-equality-gated tests

gate.description: Cosine floor the test enforces (class=approx), if known

set_equality.description: For index-select: device key set == host key set

rc.description: Overall rc (0 iff every test passed); matches the runner DONE.<rc> sentinel

content_sha256.description: SHA256 over the exact raw witness log this record was parsed from -- the diffable integrity anchor and the bridge split-fence detector
